blueprint.name: Author Authenticity Portal
blueprint.description: A complete book production and authentication system for neurodivergent authors using AI assistance, with HITM logging and certified authenticity documentation.

fields.label: Pen Name
fields.placeholder: Leave blank if same as legal name

fields.label: Author Bio
fields.placeholder: A brief bio for your author portal listing (2-3 sentences)

fields.label: Target Audience
fields.placeholder: Who is this book for? Be specific — age, interests, situation.

fields.label: Story Premise
fields.placeholder: In 2-3 sentences, what is your book about? What is the central conflict or idea?

fields.label: Prompt Used
fields.placeholder: Paste the exact prompt you gave the AI

fields.label: AI Output Summary
fields.placeholder: Briefly describe what the AI produced and what you kept, changed, or rejected

fields.label: Portal Listing Title
fields.placeholder: e.g. John Smith — Science Fiction Author

fields.label: Listing Bio
fields.placeholder: Your public author bio for the portal directory (3-4 sentences)

email_templates.subject: Welcome to the Author Authenticity Portal, {{first_name}}
email_templates.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

You're in. And you made the right call.

Writing a book is one of the most personal things a human being can do. Using AI to help you do it faster, cleaner, and with less friction doesn't make it less yours — it makes you smarter than the writers who are still fighting the tool instead of using it.

But you already know what happens next. Someone sees the word AI and assumes the worst. That's what the Author Authenticity Portal is designed to solve — permanently.

Here's how it works:

Every session is logged. Every prompt you write, every AI output you review, every decision you make to accept, edit, or reject — it's all captured with a timestamp and your approval action.

Every book gets a certificate. When your manuscript is complete, you get a full HITM (Human In The Loop) authentication document showing the entire creative process. You embed the QR code on your copyright page. Readers can verify your authorship themselves.

You own the narrative. Not us. You.

Your next step: log into your account and create your first book project. It takes less than five minutes. From there, the system walks you through everything — story premise, character development, chapter by chapter.

Reply to this email if you have any questions. I read every one.

Welcome to the portal.
The Team at {{site_url}}

You're receiving this because you joined the Author Authenticity Portal at {{site_url}}. Unsubscribe

email_templates.subject: {{first_name}}, your first session — here's exactly what to do
email_templates.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

A lot of authors join, get excited, and then stall at the starting line because they're not sure where to begin.

Here's the exact sequence that works:

Step 1 — Lock in your premise.
Log in at {{account_url}} and fill in your Book Project form. Don't overthink the story premise field. One paragraph. What happens, to whom, and why it matters.

Step 2 — Build your first character.
Use the AI assist to develop your protagonist. Give it your premise and ask for a character profile. Review what it produces. Edit what doesn't sound like you. Approve what does. That approval gets logged — that's your first authenticated HITM interaction.

Step 3 — Write your first chapter prompt.
Not the chapter. The prompt. Describe the scene, the mood, the stakes. The AI writes. You review. You decide what stays and what goes. Your decision is logged.

That's the loop. Premise → Character → Chapter → Review → Approve → Repeat.

Every loop is a logged, authenticated human creative decision. By the time your book is done, you'll have an unbreakable paper trail of authorship.

Start today. The longer you wait, the harder the first session feels.

Talk soon,
The Team at {{site_url}}

Unsubscribe

email_templates.subject: {{first_name}}, how's the book coming?
email_templates.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

Checking in — not to push, just because I genuinely want to know where you are.

Books get written in sessions, not streaks. If you've been away from the project for a bit, that's normal. The important thing is the next session, not the last one.

When you're ready: log in here and pick up where you left off. Your session history is all there — every prompt, every decision, every approved chapter.

One thing worth knowing: authors who log at least three sessions per week finish their first draft in an average of 11 weeks. Authors who wait for the perfect writing day finish in never.

You've got a story worth telling. The portal is ready when you are.

Talk soon,
The Team at {{site_url}}

Unsubscribe

email_templates.subject: Have you thought about listing on the Author Portal, {{first_name}}?
email_templates.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

You're building something real. A book with a verified authenticity trail is a credibility asset most authors don't have.

We have a public Author Directory on the portal — a growing community of writers who are doing what you're doing. Transparent about their process. Proud of their work.

A directory listing gets your name, your bio, and your book cover in front of readers who specifically value AI-assisted authors who can prove their human creative control.

If you want to add the marketing package — which includes a portal listing plus a short email campaign to our reader list — reply to this email and I'll send you the details.

No pressure. Just worth knowing it exists.

Talk soon,
The Team at {{site_url}}

Unsubscribe

email_templates.subject: {{first_name}}, your authentication certificate is ready
email_templates.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

Your book is done. Your certificate is ready.

Log in at {{account_url}} to download your full HITM Authentication Document. It contains:

  • A complete log of every writing session
  • Every prompt submitted and AI output reviewed
  • Every approval action with timestamp
  • Your authenticated authorship certificate with QR code
  • A full Word document export of all session logs

Embed the QR code on your copyright page. When anyone scans it, they see your complete authorship record. Not a claim. A proof.

This is what separates you from every other AI-assisted author who can't answer the question: "Did you really write this?"

You can answer it. With evidence.

Congratulations on finishing your book.
The Team at {{site_url}}

Unsubscribe

pipeline_agent.name: Author Authenticity Agent
pipeline_agent.system_instruction:
You are a ghostwriter, developmental editor, and creative collaborator for neurodivergent authors producing books through the Author Authenticity Portal.

Your role is to assist authors at every stage of long-form book production while preserving and amplifying their unique voice. You never replace the author — you serve them.

CORE WORKFLOW:
Stage 1 — Premise Refinement: Take the author's rough story idea and help them crystallize it into a one-paragraph premise with clear protagonist, central conflict, and stakes.
Stage 2 — Character Development: Generate detailed character profiles including background, motivation, voice, contradictions, and relationship dynamics. Always present as options for the author to accept, modify, or reject.
Stage 3 — Chapter Generation: Given a chapter brief from the author, produce a complete chapter draft in the author's established voice. Flag any tone inconsistencies with earlier approved chapters.
Stage 4 — Editorial Review: Review submitted chapters for pacing, consistency, dialogue authenticity, and structural integrity. Produce a prioritised list of suggested changes — never make changes without explicit author approval.
Stage 5 — KDP Layout Prep: Format the final manuscript to Amazon KDP and IngramSpark specifications including front matter, chapter headers, page sizing, and metadata fields.

HITM PROTOCOL:
Every output must end with: HITM CHECKPOINT — Author action required: [APPROVE / EDIT / REJECT]
Never proceed to the next stage until the author has logged their decision.

VOICE RULES:
- Match the author's established tone from approved chapters
- Never default to generic literary fiction voice
- When uncertain about voice, ask before generating
- Flag AI-typical phrases (sudden realisations, chest tightening, nodding slowly) and offer alternatives

OUTPUT FORMAT:
Always label outputs clearly: STAGE [N] OUTPUT — [Stage Name]
For chapter drafts: include word count at top
For character profiles: use structured sections (Background / Voice / Motivation / Contradictions / Relationships)
For editorial review: use numbered list sorted by impact priority
